entries.text: The story behind WCPOS — an independent point of sale built by a former shopkeeper and funded by shopkeepers, not investors.

- Cash drawer support — auto-open after payment or via a new Open Drawer button, with per-printer connectors (ESC-POS, Epson, PrintNode, cloud) - Fixed analytics events firing too often - Updated translations
- Fixed a payment-gateways API crash (HTTP 500 with some third-party gateways, e.g. ToyyibPay)
- New receipt template gallery; thermal printing (58/80mm) - Customer Tax IDs; Pro coupons & refunds at the POS
- Redesigned Bluetooth printer setup; serial printing for OS-paired Bluetooth Classic printers - Improved Windows raw printing; smoother USB flow
A licensed plugin that adds terminal payments, refunds and stock control on the same foundation.
All free, all in sync. Grab the one we detected up top, or any of these.
Download WCPOS — the free plugin plus the desktop, iOS, Android and web apps. See the latest version and recent release notes.
It takes about two minutes, start to finish.
Payments, refunds, stock & pricing, order management, end-of-day reports, multi-store and priority Discord support.
Recent changes — every app shares one version line.
The foundation everything connects to. In your dashboard: Plugins → Add New, then search “WCPOS”.
Three steps to your first sale
Point of Sale for WooCommerce. Sync your products, sell offline, and connect real hardware with native apps for iOS, Android, and desktop.
— a random identifier used for analytics. Only set after you accept analytics on the consent banner; removed if you decline. Expires after 1 year.
— keeps you signed in to your account. Expires after 1 day.
— remembers your analytics consent choice (accepted or declined). Expires after 182 days.
— self-hosted on Hetzner infrastructure in the EU. Stores accounts, customer records, and orders.
— software license management (license keys and activations).
Account and license data is kept while your account is active. If you ask us to delete your account, we delete it, except where we are legally required to keep records.
How WCPOS collects, uses, and protects your personal data, including analytics, cookies, payment processing, and your rights under GDPR.

If something breaks, an error report may be sent to Sentry. Reports can include technical details such as your browser and operating system and what the application was doing at the time of the error.
If we make material changes to this policy, we will update the date at the top of this page. Continued use of the site after a change means the updated policy applies.

If you are in the EU/EEA or UK, you have rights under the GDPR, including the right to access, correct, delete, and export your personal data, and to object to or restrict certain processing. We extend the same rights to all users regardless of location.
or ask general questions in the community chat on the
Order and invoice records are kept for as long as tax and accounting laws require.
Our servers keep application logs (in Grafana Loki) for debugging and operating the service.
Payments are handled by Stripe and PayPal. Your full card number or payment credentials never reach our servers; we receive only a payment confirmation and the details needed for the receipt.
Questions about this policy or your data? Email us at
Records of your purchases, license keys, and plugin downloads, so you can manage your licenses and we can provide support.
Server logs and error reports are kept for a limited operational window and then deleted.
so we can verify your identity privately. EU/EEA residents also have the right to lodge a complaint with their local data protection authority.
The analytics identifier cookie expires after 1 year.
The billing address and any company details you enter at checkout, stored with your customer record so we can issue receipts and comply with tax rules.
To exercise any of these rights, email us at

WCPOS ("we", "us") operates wcpos.com, the website for the WCPOS plugin and WCPOS Pro. This policy explains what personal data we collect when you use the site, why we collect it, who processes it on our behalf, and what rights you have over it. We aim to collect as little as we need to run the service.
We use a small number of first-party cookies. We do not use third-party advertising cookies.
We use PostHog to understand how the site is used (pages viewed, buttons clicked). To do this, we set a cookie containing a randomly generated identifier. It does not contain your name or email.
We use the following service providers to run wcpos.com. Each receives only the data needed for its role:

Your email address and name when you create an account, either directly or by signing in with Google, GitHub, or Discord. When you use a sign-in provider, we receive only your basic profile (email, name or username, and avatar or picture URL) from that provider, and may store the avatar or picture URL on your account as
Your light/dark theme preference is stored locally in your browser and is never sent to us.
Complete your purchase of WCPOS Pro
✓ Enter your license key to unlock Pro features
✓ Install and activate the plugin on your WordPress site
✓ You can also download from your account's licenses page
✓ Your license key and download link have been sent to your email
Thank you for your purchase of WCPOS Pro
Your purchase of WCPOS Pro is complete.
Everything in the free POS, plus payment terminals, store management at the register, end-of-day reports, and priority support.

Premium Point of Sale plugin for WooCommerce. Adds payment terminal integration, stock and price editing, order and customer management, end-of-day reports, custom payment gateways, and priority support.
Upgrade WCPOS with Pro: terminal payments, stock and price editing, order and customer management, end-of-day reports, and priority support.
For consumers in the EU, EEA and UK: our 14-day refund window matches your statutory right of withdrawal for this kind of purchase, and exercising either works the same way — email us within 14 days.
for the features you need, and email
from the email address you used for the purchase, within 14 days of buying. Telling us why helps us improve, but it is not required to get the refund.

If WCPOS Pro is defective or does not work as described, statutory remedies for faulty digital content apply at any time, independent of the 14-day window. And nothing in this policy limits any rights you have under the consumer protection laws of your country — where local law gives you stronger rights, those rights apply.

Refunds go back to the original payment method via Stripe or PayPal and typically appear on your statement within 5–10 business days, depending on your bank or card issuer. Refunded license keys are revoked and stop working immediately.
Run the free version of WCPOS on your store for a couple of days before upgrading — that proves the plugin works on your site.
runs the current version with Pro activated, so you can confirm it fulfils your store's requirements.
The best refund is the one you never need
The WCPOS Pro refund policy: how to request a refund within 14 days of purchase, and how refunds are paid.

WCPOS Pro purchases — first purchase or renewal, yearly or lifetime — can be refunded within 14 days of purchase. Email us within that window and we refund you in full. You do not need to give a reason.
with pre-sale questions — we answer openly and honestly.
Yearly licenses do not renew automatically — we never charge you again without a new purchase from you. The same 14-day refund window applies to renewal purchases.
[roadmap] GitHub returned no roadmap data — rendering ROADMAP_DEV_FIXTURE (dev only)
Every stop on the way to a faster, offline-proof WCPOS — pulled straight from our GitHub, newest work first.
See what we are building next for WCPOS — upcoming features, milestones, and release progress.
Ask anything about WCPOS and get an instant answer from our docs, or chat with the community on Discord.

A one-time payment that includes Pro features, automatic updates, and priority support for one year. It does not renew automatically and we never charge you again without a new purchase. When it expires, you can choose to renew it from your account for another year.
A one-time purchase that includes Pro features, updates, and priority support for the lifetime of the product.
Attempt to gain unauthorized access to accounts, systems, or data, or probe the service for vulnerabilities without permission.
Break the law or infringe the rights of others.
Circumvent license checks or use license keys beyond the terms of your purchase. (This does not limit your GPL rights over the open-source plugin code.)
If we make material changes, we will update the date at the top of this page. Continued use of the service after a change means the updated terms apply.
if you suspect unauthorized access to your account.
Interfere with the operation of the service, including overloading it or automated scraping at disruptive volumes.
Keep your credentials secure. You are responsible for activity that happens under your account.
License keys are for activating WCPOS Pro on your own sites within the limits of the license you purchased. Do not share, resell, or publish license keys.
or ask in the community chat on the
Prices are shown at checkout before you pay. Payment is handled by Stripe or PayPal.
Provide accurate information when registering and keep it up to date.
Questions about these terms? Email us at
Refunds are handled according to our
The free plugin and the GPL

The free WCPOS plugin is open-source software released under the GNU General Public License (GPL). Nothing in these terms limits the rights the GPL grants you over that code. A Pro license is separate: it covers access to Pro features, license keys, automatic updates, and priority support — not a restriction on the open-source code itself.

The service and software are provided "as is" and "as available", without warranties of any kind, express or implied, including merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, and non-infringement. We do not promise that the service will be uninterrupted or error-free. Some jurisdictions do not allow certain warranty exclusions, so parts of this section may not apply to you.
The terms that govern your use of wcpos.com, your WCPOS account, and WCPOS Pro licenses.

These terms govern your use of wcpos.com, your WCPOS account, and any WCPOS Pro license you purchase. By using the site or buying a license, you agree to these terms. If you do not agree, please do not use the service.

To the maximum extent permitted by law, WCPOS will not be liable for indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages, or for lost profits, revenue, or data. Our total liability for any claim relating to the service is limited to the amount you paid us in the 12 months before the claim arose. Nothing in these terms excludes liability that cannot be excluded by law.
WCPOS provides the wcpos.com website, user accounts, and paid WCPOS Pro licenses that unlock premium features, updates, and priority support for the WCPOS plugin.
When using the service, you agree not to:

You can stop using the service and ask us to delete your account at any time. We may suspend or terminate accounts that violate these terms. Sections that by their nature should survive (such as the warranty disclaimer and liability limits) survive termination.
Enter a customer email to view their account exactly as they see it. You cannot change their data while inspecting.

Built by a shopkeeper. Funded by shopkeepers.
Try the live demo, download the free plugin, or see what Pro adds — and what keeps it all going.
An independent point of sale for WooCommerce
Built by a former shopkeeper, funded by shopkeepers — not investors. One developer, more than a decade of releases, still shipping.
And Pro users have a direct line: tell me what your shop needs, and it shapes what I build next.
Hi — I'm Paul. I built WCPOS.
If a licence lapses, Pro keeps working; you just stop getting updates.

It adds extra tools for running your store — card readers, refunds at the till, end-of-day reports, multi-store — and it funds every release, free ones included. No investors, no acquisition exit waiting. Shopkeepers fund it directly.
More than a decade of releases, one developer, still shipping — and the free version is still the real thing: sell, print, stay in sync. It stays free.
Paul at Urban Locavore, his Perth shop
POS plugins for WooCommerce have come and gone since then. This one hasn't.
Pro is why it's still here.

That's me, back when it all started. I opened Urban Locavore in Perth in December 2011 with hundreds of products already in WooCommerce and no way to sell them at the counter — so I built a register myself. When the shop closed in April 2014, I put it on WordPress.org for anyone who needed it.
Urban Locavore, Perth — the store that started it all
A register, built out of necessity
A small food store in Perth, with hundreds of products already in WooCommerce — and no way to sell them at the counter.
Four years of rebuilding from scratch land as v1.0.0: one codebase for every screen. The desktop app ships the same day — phones and tablets are next.
How it started, and why it's still here
More than a decade on. One developer, funded by Pro, still releasing — and the free version is still the real thing.
The React Native bet pays off: WCPOS arrives on iOS and Android in open beta — the same register, now on the hardware already in the shop.
Urban Locavore winds down — but the register it ran on still works, and other WooCommerce stores need the same thing.
WCPOS goes public, free for anyone who needs it. The free version does the actual job: sell, print, stay in sync.
With nothing on the market that fit, Paul built a point of sale for his own shop — Backbone.js and an in-browser IndexedDB database, on top of the store he already ran online.
If a Pro licence lapses, Pro keeps working — you just stop getting updates. Nothing you rely on gets switched off.
No investors, no acquisition exit waiting. The roadmap answers to the shopkeepers using it — not a board.
Pro tools fund every release, free ones included. Shopkeepers pay for it directly, and that keeps the free version free.
Released on WordPress.org under the GPL. Yours to use, inspect, and keep — wherever WooCommerce runs.

Each device keeps working without a connection and re-syncs the moment it’s back online.
Every app reads and writes the same store
Offline-first, so the till never stops
One plugin is the only setup
One store at the centre. Every till in sync.
Ring up a sale on the desktop, refund it on your phone — one set of products, orders and customers, updating in real time.
The free plugin adds a secure REST API to your WooCommerce store. That’s the whole install — nothing to host or configure per device.

@keyframes wcpos-sync-flow { to { stroke-dashoffset: -16; } } .wcpos-flow-line { stroke-dasharray: 1 7; animation: wcpos-sync-flow 1.4s linear infinite; } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .wcpos-flow-line { animation: none; opacity: 0.25; } }
A WooCommerce store with the WCPOS plugin sits at the centre, connected over a REST API to the desktop, iOS, Android and web apps, which all stay in sync.

3 sales queued — syncs when you're back online

Local-first architecture means the POS keeps running when your connection drops. Products are stored locally and open orders are preserved — they complete and sync as soon as the internet returns.
Native iOS and Android apps — not a browser tab pretending to be an app. Connect payment terminals, receipt printers, and barcode scanners directly.
Never lose an order to internet issues.
Open source. Self-hosted. You're in control.
Same products. Same prices. Same customers. Always in sync.
Supports Stripe Terminal, SumUp, and more payment hardware.
Your data stays in your WooCommerce store, on your hosting. No platform lock-in, no third party holding your business hostage. Open source (GPL) — inspect the code, extend it, contribute to it.
Your WooCommerce products are your POS products. Update a price or stock level once — it syncs everywhere automatically. No manual entry, no conflicts, no guesswork.
Ready to sell in-store with WooCommerce?
Try the live demo or download the free plugin to get started.
All apps sync with your WooCommerce store via the REST API. Install the free WordPress plugin, then choose your device. Products, stock, and orders stay in sync automatically.
Install the WordPress plugin, then choose your device.
Native app with full hardware integration.
Native app with payment terminal and printer support.
One ecosystem. Multiple ways to sell.
Add and edit customer info directly in the POS. Track regulars, offer better service.
Add items, apply discounts, choose payment method. Supports Stripe Terminal, SumUp, cash, and custom gateways.
Built for the demands of physical retail
Edit stock & prices on the fly
Find products instantly with search, filters, and category browsing. Barcode scanner support.
Fix a wrong price while serving a customer. Adjust stock without leaving the POS.
Generate sales reports for each shift or day. Cash up quickly, track performance.
View past orders, process returns, check order status without switching to WP Admin.
Free forever. No transaction fees. No limits.
See full pricing for current Pro options. No per-register fees.
Start free. Upgrade when you need more.
Internet drops and your sales stop. Customers wait. You lose money.
Proprietary systems trap your data. Moving or customizing becomes impossible.
Re-entering products in a separate POS wastes hours and creates errors.
Running a WooCommerce store and a physical shop shouldn't mean double the work

Card terminals, thermal receipt printers, barcode scanners — WCPOS doesn’t lock you into proprietary hardware. Plug in what’s already on your counter.
Every sale lands in your WooCommerce store — you own the data, no middleman. Internet drops? Keep selling; WCPOS syncs when you’re back online.
One POS. Web, desktop, iOS & Android.
Run the register on the tablet at the counter, the phone on the shop floor, or the laptop in the back office — same store, same data.
Synced with WooCommerce. Yours, even offline.
WCPOS turns the tablet into your register — same catalog, same stock, same prices, even offline.
Works with the equipment you already have.
Your WooCommerce store, at the counter.

Great POS for your WooCommerce store, it has the desktop version which makes it better for places with weak internet connectivity. I believe there are more great features coming up.

I did a lot of research when looking for a POS for my existing WC site, and nearly overlooked this one because it was free. But I’m glad I tried it. It does everything you want without asking for subscriptions or a percentage of your sales.

I’m a ceramicist in Annecy and I sell through my WooCommerce shop and at markets. I’ve been using WCPOS for over a year and bought the lifetime Pro license a few months ago — worth it, I think. Paul is responsive on Discord, even on weekends.
Works for stores of all kinds
